<<Shooting the Dream>>

The director plans to shoot scenes with [address] and [location]. Each scene has a unique number of days. The total shooting days must be minimized. The constraint is no consecutive scenes from the same decade. The decades are {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, and {2010-2019}. The release years are 1994, 2004, 2007, 2012, and 2015. The optimal schedule is to alternate decades. The sequence is 1994, 2012, 2004, 2015, 2007. The total shooting days are 4 + 12 + 6 + 10 + 8 = 40 days. The constraint is satisfied. The sequence ensures no consecutive scenes from the same decade.

* * *

The budget is ₹50 lakhs. The cost per day for each scene is different. The goal is to maximize the number of completed scenes. The linear programming problem is to maximize the number of scenes with the budget constraint. The variables are the number of days for each scene. The objective function is to maximize the number of scenes. The constraints are the budget and the number of days for each scene. The optimal solution is to shoot scenes with the lowest cost per day. The maximum number of scenes is 4. The total cost is 4 + 6 + 8 + 10 = 28 lakhs. The remaining budget is 22 lakhs. The committee [decision] is to prioritize scenes with the lowest cost. The committee [approval] is required for the budget allocation. The committee [review] will evaluate the schedule. The committee [approval] ensures the budget is used efficiently.

******  
The director needs to allocate the budget efficiently. The maximum number of scenes is 3. The total cost is 4 + 6 + 10 = 20 lakhs. The remaining budget is 30 lakhs. The optimal solution is to shoot scenes with the lowest cost. The committee [approval] is required for the budget allocation. The committee [review] will evaluate the schedule. The committee [decision] is to prioritize scenes with the lowest cost. The committee [approval] ensures the budget is used efficiently. The maximum number of scenes is 3.
